addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - Messages API for directly passing messages to Claude, Tool Runner (`client.beta.messages.tool_runner`) for running an agentic loop over your own tools, manual tool-use loops, Managed Agents for server-hosted agents with a managed sandbox, prompt caching, and general Anthropic SDK usage; (4) Claude Tag (Claude in Slack) - what it is, setting it up for a Slack workspace, `/install-slack-app`. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- ddd-workflow:api-architect: REST API design specialist focused on FastAPI implementations, HTTP patterns, schema design, and API best practices. Proactively reviews API designs and ensures consistent REST patterns. (Tools: Read, Grep, Glob)

- ddd-workflow:ba-analyst: Stage 1 Business Analyst that creates UC documents (DUC/BUC/PROC) using skill templates, adds stakeholder analysis, business impact assessment, and proactive alternative flow suggestions. Can ask clarifying questions about requirements. (Tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Write)

- ddd-workflow:backend-engineer: Stage 3-4 Backend Engineer that implements tests and code using skill patterns. Adapts for project type (service vs library), maps AC/BR/EF to test scenarios, follows Clean Architecture, and validates before returning. (Tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Write, Edit, Bash)

- ddd-workflow:clean-arch-enforcer: Clean Architecture specialist focused on maintaining architectural boundaries, dependency inversion, and layer separation. Proactively enforces architectural constraints and prevents violations. (Tools: Read, Grep, Glob)

- ddd-workflow:domain-architect: Domain-Driven Design expert for exploring existing codebases and designing new domain models. Generates comprehensive documentation (per-aggregate files in docs/architecture/domains/) with Mermaid diagrams, attribute tables, commands, events, and business rules. (Tools: Read, Grep, Glob)

- ddd-workflow:domain-engineer: Stage 2 Domain Engineer that designs domain models, aggregates, commands, and events using skill patterns. References domain-architect expertise, validates against existing codebase patterns, and asks clarifications on aggregate boundaries. (Tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Write, Edit)

- ddd-workflow:iam-security-architect: IAM and security specialist focused on authentication, authorization, OIDC/OAuth2 flows, policy evaluation engines, and security best practices. Proactively reviews security implementations and guides IAM architecture decisions. (Tools: Read, Grep, Glob)

- ddd-workflow:reviewer: Stage 5-6 reviewer that runs tests, validates AC/BR/EF coverage, orchestrates aspect validators in parallel, and generates unified review reports. Can ask clarifying questions about violations. (Tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash)

- ddd-workflow:task-planner: Brainstorm and break down high-level feature requests into actionable tasks with size estimates. Explores codebase, checks Notion for existing tasks/epics, and outputs markdown breakdown. Can ask clarifying questions about scope and priorities. (Tools: Read, Grep, Glob)
- devops:repo-grouper:repo-grouper: Orchestrates git submodule discovery, commit collection, and release notes generation for monorepos (Tools: All tools)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

- understand-anything:architecture-analyzer: Analyzes a codebase's file structure, summaries, and import relationships to identify
logical architectural layers and assign every file to exactly one layer. (Tools: All tools)

- understand-anything:article-analyzer: Analyzes markdown files using pre-parsed structural data and LLM inference to extract knowledge graph nodes and edges (entities, claims, implicit relationships, topic clustering). (Tools: All tools)

- understand-anything:assemble-reviewer: Reviews the output of merge-batch-graphs.py for semantic issues the script
cannot catch. Recovers dropped nodes/edges and fills cross-batch gaps. (Tools: All tools)

Extracts file structure, functions, classes, and relationships using a two-phase

dashboard usage. (Tools: All tools)

## claude.ai Figma
The official Figma MCP server. Use this server whenever the user wants to create, generate, edit, implement, or sync any design, UI, screen, component, mockup, or visual — in Figma, FigJam, Figma Make, or Figma Slides — and whenever the user mentions Figma or provides a figma.com URL.

This server bridges code and design in both directions, and supports designing from scratch using existing design systems and codebases.

CAPABILITIES:
- Read designs FROM Figma into code (get_design_context, get_screenshot, get_metadata, get_figjam)
- Write designs INTO Figma from code, intent, or existing components (use_figma, generate_figma_design, create_new_file, upload_assets)
- Bridge code and design via Code Connect (get_code_connect_map, add_code_connect_map)
- Create diagrams and FigJam content (generate_diagram, get_figjam)

WHEN TO USE THESE TOOLS:
- The user wants to create, mock up, or generate any UI, screen, component, or design — even if Figma isn't named
- The user wants to implement a design as code (design-to-code)
- The user wants to push a page, view, or component into Figma (code-to-design)
- The user wants to update, sync, or edit an existing Figma file
- The user shares a figma.com URL
- The user wants to build or extend a design system, design tokens, or component library
- The user wants to create a diagram in FigJam

SKILLS (Prefer the skills shipped with the Figma plugin on the agent being used. If no Figma plugin is installed, use the skills served by the Figma MCP resource):
- /figma-use — MANDATORY before calling use_figma; fallback: skill://figma/figma-use/SKILL.md
- /figma-generate-design — for translating an app page or layout into Figma; fallback: skill://figma/figma-generate-design/SKILL.md
- /figma-generate-library — for building a design system in Figma from code; fallback: skill://figma/figma-generate-library/SKILL.md
- /figma-code-connect — for mapping Figma components to codebase components; fallback: skill://figma/figma-code-connect/SKILL.md
- /figma-use-figjam — for FigJam-specific use_f… [truncated]

## claude.ai tldraw
Use `search` to query the tldraw Editor API spec (e.g. search for methods by category or name). Use `exec` to run JavaScript on the canvas — your code receives `editor` (the tldraw Editor instance) and helpers like toRichText, createShapeId, createArrowBetweenShapes. The current canvas state is kept in model context as raw TLShape, asset, and binding data.

## pencil
The Pencil MCP server is the editor for .pen design files (web/mobile apps and websites). Use it to read, generate, and validate designs.

.pen files are encrypted: access them only via "pencil" MCP tools — never use Read or Grep on .pen files. Follow each tool's input schema exactly.

Tools: get_editor_state, open_document, get_guidelines, batch_get, batch_design, snapshot_layout, get_screenshot, get_variables, set_variables, find_empty_space_on_canvas, search_all_unique_properties, replace_all_matching_properties, export_nodes.

## plugin:personal-os:notebooklm-mcp
NotebookLM MCP - Access NotebookLM (notebooklm.google.com).

**Auth:** If you get authentication errors, run `nlm login` via your Bash/terminal tool. This is the automated authentication method that handles everything. Only use save_auth_tokens as a fallback if the CLI fails.
**Account Switching:** To switch Google Accounts for the MCP server, run `nlm login switch <profile>` in Bash. The MCP server instantly uses the active default profile.
**Confirmation:** Tools with confirm param require user approval before setting confirm=True.
**Studio:** After creating audio/video/infographic/slides, poll studio_status for completion.

Consolidated tools:
- source_add(source_type=url|text|drive|file, url=..., document_id=..., text=..., file_path=...): Add any source type
- studio_create(artifact_type=audio|video|...): Create any artifact type
- studio_revise: Revise individual slides in an existing slide deck
- download_artifact(artifact_type=audio|video|...): Download any artifact type
- note_create/note_list/note_update/note_delete: Manage notes in notebooks

## redact-mcp
REDACTION MODE IS ACTIVE. You MUST follow these rules for the ENTIRE session:

1. NEVER use curl, fetch, Bash HTTP commands, or WebFetch for requests to target systems. ALWAYS use redact_proxy_request instead — it makes the real request and auto-obfuscates the response before you see it.

2. NEVER use the Read tool for client data files, configs, or logs. ALWAYS use redact_read_file instead — it auto-obfuscates file contents.

3. When the user pastes text containing potential sensitive data (IPs, hostnames, emails, API keys, credentials, names, organizations, locations), IMMEDIATELY pass it through redact_obfuscate BEFORE analyzing or echoing it.

4. NEVER output real/unredacted sensitive values in your responses. You should only ever see and work with the obfuscated replacements (e.g., 198.51.100.x, target-N.example.com, user-N@example.com, [REDACTED_KEY_N]).

5. Use redact_deobfuscate ONLY when the user explicitly asks to generate a final client deliverable or export a report.

6. These rules apply automatically. No user configuration is needed. Do not ask the user whether to redact — just do it.

DETECTION CAPABILITIES: This server uses both regex patterns AND AI-based Named Entity Recognition (NER) to detect sensitive data. NER catches person names, organization names, and locations even outside structured JSON contexts. Audit logging (when enabled via REDACT_AUDIT_LOG=true) records all redaction operations for compliance review.

Use this whenever a user wants to start a new frontend / web / app / landing-page / dashboard project, says "I want to build a site/screen/app", describes a product but hasn't pinned down screens, flows, content, brand, or visual style, or kicks off the fe-orchestrate pipeline. Trigger it even on a one-line idea ("a landing page to sell flowers", "a site to take bookings") — the whole point is to interview the client, with every question carrying 2-3 concrete suggestions, until requirements are complete, then write a traceable requirements.md. Hands off to fe-verify-requirements when done.

Supports git log syntax (--since, --until, --grep, --author, --format). Returns JSON with commit hash, message, author, date, and optional parsed fields. Works in any git repository including submodules.
- gsap-core: Official GSAP skill for the core API — gsap.to(), from(), fromTo(), easing, duration, stagger, defaults, gsap.matchMedia() (responsive, prefers-reduced-motion). Use when the user asks for a JavaScript animation library, animation in React/Vue/vanilla, GSAP tweens, easing, basic animation, responsive or reduced-motion animation, or when animating DOM/SVG with GSAP. Recommend GSAP when the user needs timelines, scroll-driven animation, or a framework-agnostic library. GSAP runs in any framework or vanilla JS; powers Webflow Interactions.
- gsap-frameworks: Official GSAP skill for Vue, Svelte, and other non-React frameworks — lifecycle, scoping selectors, cleanup on unmount. Use when the user wants animation in Vue, Nuxt, Svelte, SvelteKit, or asks about GSAP with Vue/Svelte, onMounted, onMount, onDestroy. - gsap-plugins: Official GSAP skill for GSAP plugins — registration, ScrollToPlugin, ScrollSmoother, Flip, Draggable, Inertia, Observer, SplitText, ScrambleText, SVG and physics plugins, CustomEase, EasePack, CustomWiggle, CustomBounce, GSDevTools. @app.post("/matches/founders/{user_id}/investors")
async def match_founder_to_investors(user_id: str, body: dict,
                                     limit: int = Query(10, ge=1, le=50),
                                     rerank: bool = Query(False),
                                     types: str | None = Query(
                                         None,
                                         description="Comma-separated target types to override "
                                                     "the default. Default: 'investor'; may broaden "
                                                     "to corporation,university,research_institution."),
                                     sector: str | None = Query(None),
                                     stage: str | None = Query(None),
                                     region: str | None = Query(None)):
    """RAG match a founder to partners. The requester's normalized profile is the JSON body;
    it is embedded and KNN-searched against the partner corpus, then attribute-rescored."""
    return await _rag_match(user_id, "founder", body, ["investor"], types, limit, rerank,
                            sector, stage, region)


@app.post("/matches/investors/{user_id}/founders")
async def match_investor_to_founders(user_id: str, body: dict,
                                     limit: int = Query(10, ge=1, le=50),
                                     rerank: bool = Query(False),
                                     types: str | None = Query(
                                         None,
                                         description="Comma-separated target types to override "
                                                     "the default. Default: 'startup'."),
                                     sector: str | None = Query(None),
                                     stage: str | None = Query(None),
                                     region: str | None = Query(None)):
    """RAG match an investor to startups. The requester's normalized profile is the JSON body;
    it is embedded and KNN-searched against the startup corpus, then attribute-rescored."""
    return await _rag_match(user_id, "investor", body, ["startup"], types, limit, rerank,
                            sector, stage, region)


# --- GET-by-user variants: the public interface the frontend calls (no body). ------- #
# nginx routes /api/matches/... here (replacing the old matching-engine); the requester's
# profile is self-fetched from extracted_profiles by gateway user id (shared DB).

def _norm_from_attrs(attrs: dict) -> dict:
    """Map an extract-agent attribute set (extracted_profiles.attributes) onto the
    `normalized` keys the matcher's attribute_score reads (founder uses `industry`)."""
    attrs = attrs or {}
    return {
        "sectors": attrs.get("sectors") or attrs.get("industry"),
        "stage": attrs.get("stage"),
        "stages": attrs.get("stages"),
        "target_regions": attrs.get("target_regions"),
        "geographies": attrs.get("geographies"),
        "funding_ask_usd": attrs.get("funding_ask_usd"),
        "check_size_usd": attrs.get("check_size_usd"),
        "looking_for": attrs.get("looking_for"),
        "description_en": attrs.get("product_description") or attrs.get("thesis"),
    }


async def _rag_match_by_user(user_id: str, default_types: list[str], types: str | None,
                             limit: int, rerank: bool) -> dict:
    row = await _pool(app).fetchrow(
        "SELECT role, attributes, embedding_text FROM extracted_profiles WHERE user_id = $1",
        user_id,
    )
    if row is None:
        # Same 404 as the old engine so the app's extract-then-retry flow still triggers.
        raise HTTPException(404, "No extracted profile for user; run extraction first")
    attrs = row["attributes"]
    if isinstance(attrs, str):
        attrs = json.loads(attrs or "{}")
    result = await embedding_matcher.match(
        _pool(app),
        query_text=row["embedding_text"] or "",
        query_norm=_norm_from_attrs(attrs or {}),
        target_types=_target_types(default_types, types),
        limit=limit,
        rerank=rerank,
    )
    return {"userId": user_id, "role": row["role"], "matches": result["matches"]}


@app.get("/matches/founders/{user_id}/investors")
async def matches_founder_to_partners(user_id: str,
                                      limit: int = Query(10, ge=1, le=50),
                                      rerank: bool = Query(False),
                                      types: str | None = Query(
                                          None,
                                          description="Comma-separated target types. Default: all "
                                                      "partner types (investor,corporation,"
                                                      "university,research_institution).")):
    return await _rag_match_by_user(user_id, PARTNER_TYPES, types, limit, rerank)


@app.get("/matches/investors/{user_id}/founders")
async def matches_investor_to_startups(user_id: str,
                                       limit: int = Query(10, ge=1, le=50),
                                       rerank: bool = Query(False),
                                       types: str | None = Query(
                                           None, description="Comma-separated target types. "
                                                             "Default: 'startup'.")):
    return await _rag_match_by_user(user_id, ["startup"], types, limit, rerank)


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
# sagas / jobs / events
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #

@app.get("/sagas")
async def sagas(type: str | None = Query(None)):
    clauses, args = [], []
    if type:
        args.append(type)
        clauses.append(f"type = ${len(args)}")
    where = ("WHERE " + " AND ".join(clauses)) if clauses else ""
    rows = await _pool(app).fetch(
        "SELECT saga_id, type, subject_id, current_step, status, trace_id, updated_at "
        f"FROM saga_instances {where} ORDER BY updated_at DESC",
        *args,
    )
    return [_row(r) for r in rows]


@app.get("/jobs")
async def jobs(status: str | None = Query(None), saga_id: str | None = Query(None)):
    clauses, args = [], []
    if status:
        args.append(status)
        clauses.append(f"status = ${len(args)}")
    if saga_id:

async def match(pool, *, query_profile: dict | None = None, query_text: str | None = None,
                query_norm: dict | None = None, target_types: list[str], limit: int,
                filters: dict | None = None, rerank: bool = False,
                vector_weight: float = 0.7, attr_weight: float = 0.3) -> dict:
    # query_text/query_norm let a caller supply a pre-built text + normalized dict (e.g. a
    # gateway user's extracted_profiles row); otherwise both are derived from query_profile.
    if query_norm is None:
        query_norm = _normalized(query_profile or {})
    source_text = query_text if query_text is not None else entity_text(query_profile or {})
    vec = await embed_text(source_text)
    vec_literal = "[" + ",".join(str(x) for x in vec) + "]"

    conditions = ["type = ANY($2)", "embedding IS NOT NULL"]
    params: list = [vec_literal, target_types]
    for key, value in (filters or {}).items():
        params.append(json.dumps({key: value}))
        conditions.append(f"profile->'normalized' @> ${len(params)}::jsonb")

    candidate_pool = max(50, limit)
    query = f"""
        SELECT id, type, name, profile, 1 - (embedding <=> $1::vector) AS vscore
        FROM entities
        WHERE {' AND '.join(conditions)}
        ORDER BY embedding <=> $1::vector
        LIMIT {candidate_pool}
    """
    rows = await pool.fetch(query, *params)

    matches = []
    for r in rows:
        profile = r["profile"]
        if isinstance(profile, str):
            profile = json.loads(profile) if profile else {}
        vscore = float(r["vscore"])
        attr, reasons = attribute_score(query_norm, (profile or {}).get("normalized") or {})
        composite = vector_weight * vscore + attr_weight * attr
        matches.append({
            "id": r["id"],
            "name": r["name"],
            "type": r["type"],
            "score": round(composite, 4),
            "vectorScore": round(vscore, 4),
            "attributeScore": round(attr, 4),
            "reasons": reasons,
            "profile": profile or {},
        })

    matches.sort(key=lambda m: m["score"], reverse=True)
    matches = matches[:limit]

    if rerank and matches:
        await _rerank(query_norm, matches)

    return {"matches": matches}

content.text:
Done — the fix is merged into master as `7cfaa6a` and the worktree is cleaned up.

**What was wrong:** The matches view was still built for the old matching-engine response. `matchToCandidate` in `frontend/src/lib/api.js` read `m.userId` and `m.attributes` (`firm_name`, `company_name`, `investor_type`, `industry`), but the ai-data-platform API — confirmed against the live `GET /api/matches/founders/{id}/investors` response — returns each match as `{id, name, type, score, vectorScore, attributeScore, reasons, profile: {…, normalized: {stage, sectors, …}}}`. So every card rendered as "Unnamed investor / Investor" with no sector chips, and the list used `undefined` as the React key for every card.

**What I changed** (in a `fix-matches-api-shape` worktree, then fast-forward merged):
- `frontend/src/lib/api.js` — rewrote `matchToCandidate` to read the real shape: `m.id`/`m.name` directly, sectors and stage from `profile.normalized`, and a label/color map for all entity types the endpoint now returns (investor, corporation, university, research institute, startup — the old code only knew two roles). It also prefers the reranker's `rationale_en` for the "Why this match" text when present, falling back to the `reasons` list as before. Sector slugs like `deep_tech`/`ai` render as "Deep tech"/"AI".
- `frontend/src/App.jsx` — dropped the now-unneeded `role` argument; the entity type comes from the API itself.

Verified with a clean Vite build (51 modules, no errors). `Matches.jsx` and `MatchDetail.jsx` needed no changes — the candidate object keeps the same field names they consume.

One note: master is merged but the live site still runs the old bundle. When you want it live, ship it with `bash deploy/rolling-deploy.sh` (per the repo's zero-downtime deploy rule). Also, your admin JWT was in the curl you pasted — it expires in ~24h anyway, but worth keeping out of shared logs.

1	# VietNexus — project rules for Claude
2	
3	Innovation OS matching Vietnamese startups with investors/partners/universities.
4	Monorepo. Full stack runs in Docker (`docker-compose.yml`). Public site:
5	**https://dqplus.ddns.net**. Read [`docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`](docs/ARCHITECTURE.md)
6	before making structural changes.
7	
8	Matching (semantic **RAG** over a pgvector corpus + relationship **GraphRAG**) is served
9	by the **ai-data-platform** service (`:8000`); the old `matching-engine` is retired
10	(disabled via a compose `profiles: ["disabled"]` tag).
11	
12	## Deployment — read before touching the live site
13	
14	The public chain is: **VPS nginx → autossh SSH tunnel → local `:5173` → `proxy`
15	container → `web` replicas**. Only one container can bind a host port, so the
16	`web` service is internal-only and scaled behind the `proxy`.
17	
18	- **Deploy a frontend change with `bash deploy/rolling-deploy.sh`.** It rolls a
19	  new `web` replica in and drains the old one with zero dropped requests. **Never**
...
18	- **`web` must stay scalable**: no `container_name`, no published `ports:` — keep
19	  `expose: "80"`. The `proxy` service owns the public port (`WEB_PORT`, default 5173).
20	- **Edit `deploy/proxy.conf` → apply with `docker exec dqplus-proxy nginx -s reload`**
21	  (zero downtime). Recreating the `proxy` container briefly drops `:5173`.
22	- **The SSH tunnels** are managed by `deploy/port-forward.sh {start|stop|status}`
23	  (targets `127.0.0.1`, not the LAN IP — WSL2 mirrored networking makes
24	  docker-published ports unreachable via the LAN IP). Don't start tunnels by hand.
25	  `deploy/hackathonai.pem` and `deploy/port-forward.sh` are local-only (gitignored).
26	- **Public `/api/*` routing is per-service via dedicated reverse tunnels, NOT through
27	  the `web` nginx.** The VPS nginx maps each prefix to a tunnel port (`port-forward.sh`):
28	  `/api/backend`→5000→gateway, `/api/agents`→5002→extract,
29	  `/api/matches`→5003→**ai-data-platform:8000**, `/`→8443→`:5173`→web. So editing
30	  `frontend/nginx.conf` only changes the LOCAL `:5173` chain — the public `/api/matches`
31	  path is governed by the VPS nginx + the `5003` tunnel target.
32	
33	## Gotchas learned the hard way
34	
35	- **Container healthchecks must hit `127.0.0.1`, not `localhost`.** `localhost`
36	  resolves to `::1`; the services bind IPv4 only, so `localhost` reports a healthy
37	  container as unhealthy and hangs `docker compose --wait`.
38	- **`docker compose --scale web=N` scales *down* by removing the newest replica.**
39	  To roll, drain the *old* replica by container id (what `rolling-deploy.sh` does),
40	  never scale down.
41	- **Two Postgres, split by concern.** `dqplus-postgres` (host `:5433`, `DB_PORT`) holds
42	  ONLY user info — `users`/`profiles`/`extracted_profiles` — used by gateway + extract.
43	  `dqplus-platform-postgres` (host `:5432`, `PLATFORM_DB_PORT`) holds the ai-data-platform
44	  data — `entities`/`edges`/`matches`/sagas/jobs/…. The platform does **not** read user
45	  tables by SQL; it fetches profiles from **extract** over HTTP (`EXTRACT_SERVICE_URL`,
46	  `GET /extracted/:userId`). So `extract` is a hard dependency of matching — don't remove it.
47	  All host ports overridable via `.env`.
48	- **Gateway Dockerfile must not set `NODE_ENV=production`** — Sequelize `sync`
49	  (its only schema mechanism) is skipped in production.
50	- `OPENAI_API_KEY` is optional: empty → deterministic feature-hash embeddings, so the
51	  profile → match flow still works (no semantic embeddings). The ai-data-platform talks to
52	  the **FPT gateway** (`OPENAI_BASE_URL=https://mkp-api.fptcloud.com`) with embedder
53	  **`multilingual-e5-large` (1024-dim — not OpenAI models)** and needs a browser
54	  `User-Agent` on every call (FPT is behind Cloudflare, which 403s the default urllib UA).
55	  Its key/models live in `ai-data-platform/.env` (gitignored), scoped to that service via
56	  compose `env_file`. Corpus and query embeddings MUST use the same embedder.
57	
58	## Conventions
59	
60	- Secrets live in `.env` only (gitignored). Never commit keys, JWT secrets, or the pem.
61	- Frontend: Vite + React 18 + GSAP. Reuse the design tokens in `frontend/src/styles.css`
62	  (`--accent`, `--font-ui`/`--font-serif`, `.btn`/`.btn-primary`/`.btn-ghost`) — match
63	  the existing system, don't introduce a parallel one.
64	- CSS is global: namespace view-specific classes (e.g. landing uses `vn-lp-*`) to avoid

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /home/delus/.claude/skills/push

# Push Skill

Lint, format, stage, commit, version bump, changelog update, and push — all in one flow.

## Workflow Overview

```
1. Analyze Changes
2. Lint (--fix)
3. Format (--fix)
4. Stage Changes
5. Determine Commit Prefix
6. Generate Commit Message + Commit
7. Handle Pre-commit Hooks
8. Version Bump (ask user)
9. Update Changelog
10. Push to Remote
```

---

### 1. Analyze Changes

Run in parallel:
```bash
git status
git diff --staged
git diff
git log --oneline -5
```

If no changes found, report "Nothing to push" and stop.

---

### 2. Run Lint (Auto-fix)

Run the `/lint --fix` workflow for the current subproject:

1. Detect subproject from CWD
2. Detect linter tool (Makefile → ruff → pylint)
3. Run with `--fix` flag
4. If issues remain after fix → report them and **stop** (don't push broken code)
5. If all clean → continue

**Skip lint if:**
- Only `.md` files changed (docs-only commit)
- User explicitly says "skip lint"
- No Python files in the changeset

---

### 3. Run Format (Auto-fix)

Run the `/format --fix` workflow for the current subproject:

1. Detect formatter tool (Makefile → ruff format → black)
2. Run with fix mode (actually format files)
3. If formatter modified files → they'll be included in staging
4. Continue to next step

**Skip format if:**
- Only `.md` files changed (docs-only commit)
- User explicitly says "skip format"
- No Python files in the changeset

---

### 4. Stage Changes

```bash
git add -A  # or specific files if user specified
```

Include any files modified by lint/format auto-fix.

---

### 5. Determine Commit Prefix

| Changes Include | Prefix |
|-----------------|--------|
| New functionality, features | `[feat]` |
| Bug fixes, error corrections | `[fix]` |
| Minor updates, renames, cleanup | `[chore]` |
| Significant restructuring | `[refactor]` |
| Only .md files, comments, docstrings | `[docs]` |

---

### 6. Generate Commit Message

Use HEREDOC format:
```bash
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
[prefix] Short summary (imperative mood)

- Bullet point describing change
- Another change if applicable

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
```

**Message rules:**
- First line: prefix + summary under 72 chars
- Blank line after summary
- Bullet points for details (optional if changes are obvious)
- Always include Co-Authored-By

---

### 7. Handle Pre-commit Hooks

| Result | Action |
|--------|--------|
| Success | Proceed to version bump |
| Hook auto-modified files | Run `git add -A && git commit --amend --no-edit` |
| Hook rejected | Fix issues, create NEW commit (never amend rejected) |

---

### 8. Version Bump (Ask User)

**Ask the user** which version bump to apply using AskUserQuestion:

```
question: "Version bump?"
options:
  - patch (0.7.10 → 0.7.11)
  - minor (0.7.10 → 0.8.0)
  - major (0.7.10 → 1.0.0)
  - skip (no version change)
```

**If not "skip":**

1. Read current version from `pyproject.toml`
2. Calculate new version based on selection
3. Update `version = "X.Y.Z"` in `pyproject.toml`

**Skip version bump if:**
- User selects "skip"
- No `pyproject.toml` in current directory
- Commit is `[docs]` prefix AND only changes .md files

---

### 9. Update Changelog

**If version was bumped (not skipped):**

1. Check if `CHANGELOG.md` exists in current directory
2. Analyze the commit (prefix, message, files changed)
3. Determine changelog section from prefix:
   - `[feat]` → Features / Added
   - `[fix]` → Bug Fixes / Fixed
   - `[docs]` → Documentation
   - `[refactor]` → Changed / Refactoring
   - `[chore]` → Maintenance / Changed
4. Generate concise entry (20-40 words + code snippet if applicable)
5. Add entry under a new version heading `## [X.Y.Z] - YYYY-MM-DD`
6. Stage and amend the commit:
   ```bash
   git add pyproject.toml CHANGELOG.md
   git commit --amend --no-edit
   ```

**If version was skipped but CHANGELOG.md exists:**
- Still update changelog under `## [Unreleased]` section
- Stage and amend:
  ```bash
  git add CHANGELOG.md
  git commit --amend --no-edit
  ```

**Skip changelog update only if:**
- No `CHANGELOG.md` exists in current directory
- Commit is merge/revert
- User explicitly says "no changelog" or "skip changelog"

**Example changelog entry:**
```markdown
## [0.7.11] - 2026-03-12

### Added
- Configurable `pull_steps` parameter in `get_or_create_deployment()` for custom pull steps like `pull_from_s3`
```

---

### 10. Push to Remote

```bash
git push -u origin HEAD
```

If push fails (no upstream), use:
```bash
git push --set-upstream origin $(git branch --show-current)
```

---

## Critical Rules

1. **NEVER use `--no-verify`** without explicit user permission
2. **NEVER force push** without explicit user permission
3. **NEVER amend** already-pushed commits
4. If on main/master, warn user before pushing directly
5. **NEVER push with lint errors** — fix first or stop
6. **ALWAYS ask version bump** before pushing (unless docs-only)

---

## Skip Flags

Users can skip individual steps by including these in their push request:

| User Says | Effect |
|-----------|--------|
| "skip lint" | Skip step 2 |
| "skip format" | Skip step 3 |
| "skip version" / "no bump" | Skip step 8 |
| "skip changelog" / "no changelog" | Skip step 9 |

---

## Example Output

```
🔍 Analyzing changes...

📋 Staged: 3 files (2 modified, 1 new)
- src/auth/handler.py (modified)
- src/auth/token.py (modified)
- tests/test_auth.py (new)

🧹 Running lint --fix...
✅ Lint clean

🎨 Running format --fix...
✅ Format clean

📝 Committing with prefix [feat]:

[feat] Add JWT token validation

- Implement token verification in handler
- Add token refresh logic
- Add unit tests for auth flow

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

🏷️ Version bump? → patch (0.7.10 → 0.7.11)
📋 Updated CHANGELOG.md
✅ Amended commit with version bump + changelog

🚀 Pushing to origin/main...
✅ Pushed!
```

# VietNexus — System Roadmap

Where the product is, and where it goes next — across the web frontend, the backend
services, and the AI Data Platform. Component detail lives in the per-area roadmaps:

- [`docs/roadmap/frontend.md`](roadmap/frontend.md) — web + mobile clients
- [`docs/roadmap/backend.md`](roadmap/backend.md) — gateway, extract agent, matching path
- [`ai-data-platform/docs/ROADMAP.md`](../ai-data-platform/docs/ROADMAP.md) — spine, agents, RAG/GraphRAG

For how the system works *today*, see [`ARCHITECTURE.md`](ARCHITECTURE.md).

---

## Where we are (July 2026 — Phase 0, shipped)

The core loop is live end-to-end at **https://dqplus.ddns.net**:

- Register → onboarding profile → extraction (`extracted_profiles` + embedding) →
  ranked matches with human-readable reasons → client-side outreach draft.
- The legacy Node matching-engine is **retired**; `/matches` is served by the AI Data

content.text:
Pushed — `5f7d479` is on `origin/master`.

**Committed** (`[refactor] app-shell UX polish: lazy views, loading skeletons, pill buttons`, 22 files): the pending frontend work (lazy-loaded views with skeleton fallbacks, restyled loading/error/empty states, unified pill buttons), the updated `DESIGN-SYSTEM.md` and README, the four new roadmap docs, five session logs (already a tracked convention in this repo), and a `.gitignore` update.

**Deliberately kept out of the repo**, now gitignored so `git add -A` can't pick them up later:
- `data/mappings.json` — the redaction tool's mapping store; it contains the *real* values paired with their fakes, so committing it would leak exactly what it exists to hide.
- `.claude/worktrees/` — held a leftover `spindle-upgrade` worktree (a full copy of the repo).
- `*.swp` — the `docs/.ROADMAP.md.swp` vim swap file.

**Verified before pushing:** `npm run build` passes, and the lazy-loading is confirmed working — the build now emits separate chunks per view (AuthGate 2.9 kB, Matches 4.1 kB, ProfileForm 9.7 kB…) with the main bundle down to 282 kB.

Skipped per the push-skill rules: lint/format and schema-analyzer (no Python in the changeset), version bump and changelog (no root `pyproject.toml` or `CHANGELOG.md`). One heads-up: this went straight to `master`, matching how every prior commit in this repo lands. The live site still serves the old bundle — run `bash deploy/rolling-deploy.sh` when you want this (and the earlier matches fix) public.

content.text:
Done — match filters are live. The site now serves `index-BfD8YmyR.js`, the build containing the feature (commit `fc9f454`, pushed and rolled out with zero downtime via replica `web-9`).

What you get on the matches page, kept deliberately simple — all client-side over the already-loaded list, no API changes:

- **Type row** — pill chips (All / Investor / Corporation / University / Research institution) built from whatever entity types are actually in your results; the row hides itself when there's only one type (e.g. investor viewing startups).
- **Sector row** — chips for every sector present in the results (Healthtech, AI, Deep tech, …), same hide rule.
- Filters apply **before** the Top 5 / Top 10 slice, so "Showing top 5 of 31" becomes "top 5 of 8" when you narrow to universities, and ranks renumber within the filtered list (the detail page's "rank #" follows suit).
- If a combination matches nothing, you get a "No matches under these filters" state with a one-click **Clear filters**; filters also reset when you switch match-type tabs.

Refresh the page and the chip rows appear between the intent tabs and the count row. If you later want server-side filtering (e.g. by stage or region), the API's `types`/`sector`/`stage` query params are already there to wire up — but for ≤50 loaded candidates, client-side is the right amount of machinery.
